author: potuz
content: yes, the execution requests being invalid should invalidate the payload as they do today

author: potuz
content: Perhaps we could decouple this but I believe this imposes a condition on requests which we do not necessarily need

author: nflaig
content: what you mean by invalid? execution request can't invalidate the block

author: nflaig
content: that's how they are desinged since electra, they are just no-ops if invalid

author: potuz
content: oh crap I am being confused by something else! you're right, the requests being invalid won't need a rollback

author: potuz
content: we do invalidate on false deserialization

author: nflaig
content: you would catch that one already when you process the envelope, and would reject it, so the rollback scenario can't happen

author: potuz
content: yeah, I think this is is right

author: potuz
content: so in this design the beacon just notifies the engine of the new payload and adjusts forkchoice

author: potuz
content: but does not do anything onchain

author: potuz
content: the context of the execution requests is done before slot transition then on the next block?

author: potuz
content: and then epoch transition will be probably a problem if we keep that context

author: nflaig
content: https://gist.github.com/lodekeeper/6abbbc1f04bb1985f2f7bf165bfa0fa8 is a very rough spec of what i had in mind

author: potuz
content: right, but the issue is that the `state` is already advanced when  we get the call to process the block isn't it?

author: potuz
content: so the requests are being passed a state with a different context (which can be particularly problematic at slot 0)

author: potuz
content: and if we want to keep the previous context then implementations become a little trickier

author: nflaig
content: yes, `process_slots` needs to be called before applying execution requests to restore checkpoint state and dependent root as defined pre-gloas

author: potuz
content: yeah but this generates the problem that the requests themselves are executed in a slot context  different than when they were included.

author: potuz
content: not sure if this is really an issue though

author: nflaig
content: one edge case is that execution requests in the last slot of the epoch are delayed by one epoch, but all of these are queue based anyways

author: potuz
content: the very same requests will have different poststates depending on who builds on them

author: potuz
content: which is also kinda crazy

author: potuz
content: so the pre-state to the next block is really not deterministic until you start to build that block. So for example, a proposer will have to get the last payload at 31, precompute the epoch transition, precompute and cache the requests  (as they are now part of state transition) and then wait to the beginning of the slot to call get_head and decide where to build

author: potuz
content: if a block is missing, the next proposer needs to precompute the same requests cause their result may be different, even though no block was included

author: nflaig
content: this is valid but also is kinda why it solves the core issues brought up, I don't think there is a real practical impact

author: nflaig
content: I think this is the same as it is with the current spec

author: potuz
content: with the current spec you locked in a state and then you do the transition

author: nflaig
content: my whole design is based on the premise that the intermediate post payload state isn't required, I am still not sure about that but I don't see a difference for proposer on whether or not they build on FULL or EMPTY in current design vs. my proposal, you have to run the `state_transition` either but I don't see why you would need to run it before `get_head`?

author: potuz
content: Let's say the post block  N+1 does not include the requests. How do you validate this? This is the fundamental problem of these designs unless you commit the full requests in the bid: the state transition function is no longer state, block -> state.

author: potuz
content: if you do commit the full requests in the bid then it works, but it comes with the problem of having the requests in the bid 🙂

author: nflaig
content: that for sure doesn't work because the proposer has to trust the builder that the execution requests are valid, or you need to figure out what you do if they are invalid, like what do you do with the block, you could make it invalid retroactively when processing the payload but I think that is completely broken design

author: nflaig
content: so agree on that point 100%

author: potuz
content: I think having a state transition that is no longer state, block -> state is problematic

author: potuz
content: to say the least 😅

author: nflaig
potuz
content:
> isn't my proposal strictly better here, you can pre-compute the epoch transition without relying on the payload itself which means you can run it earlier in the previous slot (as soon as you see the beacon block and are confident it won't be reorged)
In fact this is a good thing of this approach, we do this anyway only when the payload is late.

author: potuz
content: I think the stf having a signature of state, block, optional<payload> -> state  works

author: nflaig
content: you prefer that over not modyfing that signature at all?

author: potuz
content: At this stage I prefer what we have now rather than redesigning forkchoice to see if it's safe with this signature. But I think it should work

author: potuz
content: Notice that you have it what way isn't it? You're getting the requests from the previous payload

author: nflaig
content:
I was thinking about having an optional param like you said above

but my current design just adds them to the beacon block body

```python
class BeaconBlockBody(Container):
    # ...
    signed_execution_payload_bid: SignedExecutionPayloadBid
    payload_attestations: List[PayloadAttestation, MAX_PAYLOAD_ATTESTATIONS]
    # execution requests from parent's payload (empty if parent was EMPTY)
    parent_execution_requests: ExecutionRequests
```

and updates `process_block`
```python
def process_block(state: BeaconState, block: BeaconBlock) -> None:
    # Process parent's deferred execution requests FIRST
    # (uses state.latest_block_header.slot and state.latest_execution_payload_bid
    #  which are overwritten by process_block_header and process_execution_payload_bid)
    process_parent_execution_payload(state, block)
    process_block_header(state, block)
    # ...
```

I don't think it's strictly necessary from spec perspective but I am also not sure if changing the signature of `state_transition` is a good idea since it's stable `(state, block) -> state` since phase0

in general, having everything that's needed to run stf seems like a good idea, it keeps it more self-contained

author: nflaig
content: unless I am completely missing something my proposal has no impact on fork choice

author: nflaig
content: in fact, it also fixes everything that is currently not correct in the spec like `store_target_checkpoint_state` and `is_within_weak_subjectivity_period` + restores checkpoint state and `dependent_root` semantics on the api
